The classification of Mycopteris taxifolia reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other ferns in the Polypodiaceae family.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, likely due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
